messages.content: You are a Lean 4/Mathlib context-planning agent. Prepare a compact context pack for formalizing a LaTeX theorem-like source unit. The target Lean declarations aligned to the selected source unit are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "rules": [
    "Do not infer or reveal hidden target Lean declaration names for the selected unit.",
    "Do not write theorem/lemma Lean code in target_statement_sketch; exact API syntax belongs in needed_mathlib_context and will be hydrated by tools.",
    "Do not bundle all source parts into one conjunction unless the source unit itself requires that shape.",
    "When a source theorem states a result for several cases such as upper/lower, left/right, or an `or` hypothesis, prefer separate narrow planned_declarations for each case when the checked APIs or natural Lean statements are case-specific. Do not collapse case-specific source claims into one disjunctive theorem just to reduce task count.",
    "If a broad source unit needs a construction or auxiliary lemma before the main claim can be stated/proved, split it into ordered planned_declarations with role same_unit_helper followed by role main_claim and explicit depends_on_task_ids.",
    "Same-unit helper tasks are declarations for the later generator to create from the selected source unit, not prior context. Do not use hidden aligned/referencing Lean names for them.",
    "Use previous project declarations only if they are shown under prior_project_context.",
    "Use local_file_predecessor_declarations only as same-file helper/style context; the selected unit's aligned/referencing target declarations are omitted.",
    "Do not treat Mathlib as the only context; enumerate source/project/local/Mathlib context separately.",
    "Prefer exact Mathlib names when known; otherwise give a narrow query plus expected signature shape.",
    "Keep added context tight: prefer a few thousand tokens or less per source unit."
  ],
  "schema": {
    "units": [
      {
        "context_pack_size_risk": "low|medium|high",
        "formalization_risks": [
          "source ambiguity, missing notation, broad multi-part theorem"
        ],
        "planned_declarations": [
          {
            "depends_on_task_ids": [
              "earlier same-unit helper task ids that this task should use"
            ],
            "kind": "def|theorem|lemma|instance|notation|unknown",
            "missing_or_uncertain_context": [
              "what a second lookup round should resolve"
            ],
            "needed_mathlib_context": [
              {
                "expected_signature_or_shape": "expected type/signature/docstring",
                "name_or_query": "exact Mathlib name or narrow search query",
                "why_needed": "definition/proof/tactic support"
              }
            ],
            "needed_project_context": [
              {
                "name": "previous project declaration if provided or likely needed",
                "why_needed": "supporting theorem/definition/notation"
              }
            ],
            "needed_source_context": [
              "source labels/statements"
            ],
            "role": "main_claim|same_unit_helper",
            "source_part": "whole unit or part marker",
            "target_statement_sketch": "prose mathematical target sketch; do not write exact Lean syntax, declaration names, or guessed API argument order",
            "task_id": "unit-001-task-1"
          }
        ],
        "selector_confidence": 0.0,
        "source_focus_summary": "short summary",
        "unit_key": "unit-001"
      }
    ]
  },
  "task": "For each LaTeX source unit, decompose the source into ordered Lean declaration tasks and select tight context for each task. The context inventory must be separate: source text, previous book/source statements, previous project declarations, local file/import/style context, and selected Mathlib APIs.",
  "units": [
    {
      "benchmark_policy": {
        "local_file_context_source": "prior_project_declarations_only",
        "local_file_predecessor_declaration_source": "same Lean file declarations before selected unit placement line, plus bounded transitive same-file dependencies and nearby structure extensionality support referenced by those predecessor snippets; benchmark-only placement metadata, current-unit aligned/referencing declarations omitted",
        "posthoc_alignment_hidden": true,
        "target_lean_available_to_selector": false
      },
      "local_file_context_candidates": [
        {
          "kind": "open",
          "line_range": [
            45,
            45
          ],
          "name": "open Finset BigOperators",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "reason": "Local Lean context observed around safe prior project declarations. Use as placement/style guidance; do not assume hidden target declarations.",

        },
        {

          "line_range": [
            47,

          ],
          "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SubtractiveMethods",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "reason": "Local Lean context observed around safe prior project declarations. Use as placement/style guidance; do not assume hidden target declarations.",
          "source": "file_context_from_prior_project_declaration"
        }
      ],
      "local_file_predecessor_declarations": [
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def multiplicity {n d : ℕ} (x : Fin n → Fin d) (k : Fin d) : ℕ :=\n  (univ.filter fun i => x i = k).card",
          "line_range": [
            64,
            67
          ],
          "name": "multiplicity",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def toSign (b : ZMod 2) : ℤ := if b = 0 then 1 else -1",
          "line_range": [
            83,
            85
          ],
          "name": "toSign",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def signProduct {n d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ZMod 2) (x : Fin n → Fin d) : ℤ :=\n  ∏ i : Fin n, toSign (e (x i))",
          "line_range": [
            93,
            98
          ],
          "name": "signProduct",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "lemma toSign_sq (b : ZMod 2) : toSign b ^ 2 = 1 := by\n  fin_cases b <;> simp [toSign]",
          "line_range": [
            99,
            102
          ],
          "name": "toSign_sq",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "lemma toSign_pow_odd (b : ZMod 2) (m : ℕ) (hm : Odd m) : toSign b ^ m = toSign b := by\n  obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := hm\n  calc toSign b ^ m = toSign b ^ (2 * k + 1) := by rw [hk, two_mul]\n    _ = (toSign b ^ 2) ^ k * toSign b := by ring\n    _ = 1 ^ k * toSign b := by rw [toSign_sq]\n    _ = toSign b := by ring",
          "line_range": [
            111,
            118
          ],
          "name": "toSign_pow_odd",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "lemma toSign_add_one (b : ZMod 2) : toSign (b + 1) = -toSign b := by\n  fin_cases b <;> decide",
          "line_range": [
            119,
            125
          ],
          "name": "toSign_add_one",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "theorem",
          "lean_snippet": "theorem signProduct_eq_prod_pow {n d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ZMod 2) (x : Fin n → Fin d) :\n    signProduct e x = ∏ k : Fin d, (toSign (e k)) ^ (multiplicity x k) := by\n  unfold signProduct multiplicity\n  rw [← Finset.prod_fiberwise Finset.univ x (fun i => toSign (e (x i)))]\n  congr 1\n  ext k\n  simp only [prod_filter]\n  have h1 : ∏ a : Fin n, (if x a = k then toSign (e (x a)) else 1) =\n            ∏ a : Fin n, (if x a = k then toSign (e k) else 1) := by\n    apply Finset.prod_congr rfl\n    intro a _\n    split_ifs with h\n    · rw [h]\n    · rfl\n  rw [h1]\n  rw [Finset.prod_ite]\n  simp only [prod_const_one, mul_one]\n  rw [Finset.prod_const]",
          "line_range": [
            126,
            154
          ],
          "name": "signProduct_eq_prod_pow",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def signSum {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ZMod 2) : ℤ :=\n  ∑ k : Fin d, toSign (e k)",
          "line_range": [
            155,
            157
          ],
          "name": "signSum",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        }
      ],
      "previous_source_context": [
        {
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "theorem",
            "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.tex:thm.cancel.all-even",
            "labels": [
              "thm.cancel.all-even"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              25,
              42
            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n\\label{thm.cancel.all-even}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$ and $d\\in\\mathbb{N}$. An\n$n$-tuple $\\left(  x_{1},x_{2},\\ldots,x_{n}\\right)  \\in\\left[  d\\right]  ^{n}$\nis said to be \\emph{all-even} if each element of $\\left[  d\\right]  $ occurs\nan even number of times in this $n$-tuple (i.e., if for each $k\\in\\left[\nd\\right]  $, the number of all $i\\in\\left[  n\\right]  $ satisfying $x_{i}=k$\nis even). For example, the $4$-tuple $\\left(  1,4,4,1\\right)  $ and the\n$6$-tuples $\\left(  1,3,3,5,1,5\\right)  $ and $\\left(  2,4,2,4,3,3\\right)  $\nare all-even, while the $4$-tuples $\\left(  1,2,2,4\\right)  $ and $\\left(\n2,4,6,4\\right)  $ are not.\n\nThen, the number of all all-even $n$-tuples $\\left(  x_{1},x_{2},\\ldots\n,x_{n}\\right)  \\in\\left[  d\\right]  ^{n}$ is\n\\[\n\\dfrac{1}{2^{d}}\\sum_{k=0}^{d}\\dbinom{d}{k}\\left(  d-2k\\right)  ^{n}.\n\\]\n\n\\end{theorem}"
          }
        }
      ],
      "prior_project_context": [
        {
          "project_declarations": [
            {
              "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
              "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_prior_project_declaration",
              "kind": "def",
              "lean_snippet": "def allEvenTuples (n d : ℕ) : Finset (Fin n → Fin d) :=\n  univ.filter IsAllEven",
              "line_range": [
                307,
                312
              ],
              "name": "allEvenTuples",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [
                "thm.cancel.all-even"
              ],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib"
              ],
              "kind": "def",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- An n-tuple `x : Fin n → Fin d` is \"all-even\" if each element of `Fin d`\noccurs an even number of times. For example, the 4-tuple `(1,4,4,1)` is all-even\nsince 1 appears twice and 4 appears twice.\n\nLabel: Definition from Theorem `thm.cancel.all-even` -/\ndef IsAllEven {n d : ℕ} (x : Fin n → Fin d) : Prop :=\n  ∀ k : Fin d, Even ((univ.filter fun i => x i = k).card)",
              "line_range": [
                55,
                61
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SignedCounting.SubtractiveMethods",
              "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SubtractiveMethods.IsAllEven",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [],
              "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [
                "thm.cancel.all-even"
              ],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib"
              ],
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- The main counting formula (Theorem `thm.cancel.all-even`):\nThe number of all-even n-tuples in `(Fin n → Fin d)` equals\n`(1/2^d) ∑_{k=0}^d C(d,k) (d-2k)^n`.\n\nThis formula implies that `∑_{k=0}^d C(d,k) (d-2k)^n` is nonnegative\nand divisible by `2^d`, which is not obvious from the formula itself\ndue to the alternating signs when `n` is odd.\n\nLabel: Theorem `thm.cancel.all-even` -/\ntheorem allEven_count_formula (n d : ℕ) :\n    ((allEvenTuples n d).card : ℤ) * 2^d = ∑ k ∈ range (d + 1), d.choose k * (d - 2*k : ℤ)^n",
              "line_range": [
                445,
                460
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SignedCounting.SubtractiveMethods",
              "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SubtractiveMethods.allEven_count_formula",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [],
              "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            }
          ],
          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source unit with project declarations. These declarations are prior context, not aligned target declarations for the selected unit.",
          "source_labels": [
            "thm.cancel.all-even"
          ],
          "source_unit_id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.tex:thm.cancel.all-even"
        }
      ],
      "source_context_candidates": {
        "max_previous_same_file_context": 2,
        "selected_context_refs": [
          {
            "environment": "theorem",
            "labels": [
              "thm.cancel.all-even"
            ],
            "line_range": [
              25,
              42
            ],
            "unit_id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.tex:thm.cancel.all-even"
          }
        ],
        "selection_policy": "All explicit referenced source units plus the most recent same-file predecessor units up to max_previous_same_file_context."
      },
      "source_unit": {
        "environment": "lemma",
        "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.tex:lem.cancel.all-even.l1",
        "labels": [
          "lem.cancel.all-even.l1"
        ],
        "line_range": [
          144,
          154
        ],
        "parse_warnings": [],
        "part_markers": [],
        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SignedCounting/SubtractiveMethods.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.cancel.all-even.l1}Let $n,d\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Then,%\n\\begin{align*}\n&  \\sum_{\\left(  e_{1},e_{2},\\ldots,e_{d}\\right)  \\in\\left\\{  1,-1\\right\\}\n^{d}}\\left(  e_{1}+e_{2}+\\cdots+e_{d}\\right)  ^{n}\\\\\n&  =\\sum_{\\left(  x_{1},x_{2},\\ldots,x_{n}\\right)  \\in\\left[  d\\right]  ^{n}%\n}\\ \\ \\sum_{\\left(  e_{1},e_{2},\\ldots,e_{d}\\right)  \\in\\left\\{  1,-1\\right\\}\n^{d}}e_{x_{1}}e_{x_{2}}\\cdots e_{x_{n}}.\n\\end{align*}\n\n\\end{lemma}"
      },
      "unit_key": "unit-001"
    }
  ]
}
